data.title: STOCKS | Middle East Conflict's Limited Impact on Emerging Markets
data.description: Emerging economies heavily dependent on Middle East imports have not seen their stock markets suffer as initially anticipated due to the ongoing conflict. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting that despite the geopolitical tensions, these markets have shown resilience. Analysts had predicted significant disruptions, but the financial impact has been less severe than expected. Investors remain cautious, monitoring developments closely to assess future risks. The situation underscores the complex dynamics between geopolitical events and market responses.

data.title: Vietnam's April CPI Exceeds Expectations at 5.46%
data.description: Vietnam's Consumer Price Index (CPI) for April showed a year-on-year increase of 5.46%, surpassing the anticipated rate of 4.60%, according to Jin10. The previous month's CPI was recorded at 4.65%, indicating a notable rise in inflationary pressures.

data.title: LAB fell below $1, dropping more than 50% in 4 hours.
data.description: According to GMGN data, LAB fell more than 50% in 4 hours, breaking below $1, and is currently trading at $0.98.

data.title: a16z Says 'Stablecoin' Term Outdated as Digital Dollars Rise
data.description: Andreessen Horowitz's crypto arm, a16z, argues that the term 'stablecoin' is becoming obsolete as digital dollars integrate into mainstream finance. According to BeInCrypto, the firm suggests that stability is now a prerequisite rather than a defining feature of these assets. In February 2026, stablecoins surpassed ACH in monthly volume, reaching $7.2 trillion compared to ACH's $6.8 trillion. a16z anticipates a shift towards terms like 'digital dollars' and 'on-chain assets,' reflecting their role in global payments infrastructure. The firm notes that money now behaves like software, embedded in consumer applications.

data.title: Barry Silbert Highlights $2 Trillion Global Demand for Decentralized Digital Value Storage
data.description: Barry Silbert, founder of Digital Currency Group (DCG), recently shared insights on the X platform regarding the substantial global demand for decentralized digital value storage tools like Bitcoin, estimated at $2 trillion. According to ChainCatcher, Silbert noted that the industry initially perceived Bitcoin as sufficiently private for global value storage and transfer. However, as market awareness has grown, the need for privacy has become more apparent, potentially benefiting privacy-focused cryptocurrencies like Zcash due to their 'prophetic advantage.'

data.title: OpenAI Considers Delaying IPO Amid Financial Reporting Concerns
data.description: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar has privately recommended postponing the company's initial public offering (IPO) until 2027 due to unmet financial reporting standards required for public companies. According to NS3.AI, CEO Sam Altman is eager to proceed with a listing as early as the fourth quarter of 2026. However, OpenAI has yet to initiate the formal IPO process, and insiders indicate that the company is deliberating on the timing in light of slower growth.

data.title: Ethereum's Gas Limit May Increase Significantly After Glamsterdam Upgrade
data.description: Hasufl has indicated that Ethereum's gas limit could potentially increase from 60 million to approximately 200 million following the Glamsterdam upgrade. According to NS3.AI, Hasufl also mentioned that mainnet gas fees might remain close to zero for the next few years, provided that network demand does not increase proportionately.

data.title: OpenAI's CFO has recommended postponing the IPO to 2027 due to slowing growth and failure to meet financial standards.
data.description: OpenAI's ICFO, Sarah Friar, privately suggested postponing the company's IPO to 2027, citing current financial reporting standards for publicly traded companies. CEO Sam Altman, however, wants to proceed with the IPO as soon as possible, in the fourth quarter of 2026. Sources indicate a disagreement between the two sides regarding the IPO timeline. Data shows OpenAI's growth has slowed. ChatGPT's weekly active users increased from 800 million in December 2025 to 900 million in February 2026, falling short of the previously set target of 1 billion. Meanwhile, the company's annualized revenue is approximately $25 billion, while competitor Anthropic has exceeded $30 billion. At the same time, OpenAI's computing power investment plans have also been adjusted, with internal discussions regarding the matching of future expenditures with revenue. Investment banks believe that companies that go public first will have a financial advantage in the industry, and OpenAI has not yet initiated the formal IPO process.

data.title: Iran Proposes New Negotiation Terms with the U.S.
data.description: Iran has introduced a new negotiation proposal, according to Odaily. The proposal indicates that Iran will no longer demand the U.S. lift the blockade on the Strait of Hormuz before face-to-face talks between representatives of the two countries. This development was reported by American media on the 2nd.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Drops Below 2,300 USDT with a Narrowed 0.10% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On May 03, 2026, 02:24 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 2,300 USDT and is now trading at 2,299.649902 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.10% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Bitcoin Community Reaches Consensus on Quantum Computing Threat
data.description: Alex Thorn has indicated that the Bitcoin community is forming an initial consensus regarding the potential threat posed by quantum computing. According to NS3.AI, Thorn noted that the prevailing opinion is that assets associated with Satoshi Nakamoto's P2PK addresses should remain untouched. He further explained that the risk is perceived to be lower than initially anticipated, as these holdings are distributed across approximately 22,000 addresses, each containing 50 BTC.

data.title: Dragonfly Partner Discusses Institutional Influence on Bitcoin Market
data.description:
Dragonfly partner Haseeb Qureshi has commented on the current state of the cryptocurrency market, noting a significant withdrawal of retail investors. According to Odaily, Qureshi highlighted that institutions are now forming the market's foundation and supporting Bitcoin's price. He emphasized Bitcoin's potential as a mature asset with sustained growth over the next 15 to 20 years, while altcoins need clear narratives to regain retail interest.

Qureshi also remarked that the core of the crypto industry remains centered around money and financial scenarios, with areas like DeFi, stablecoins, exchanges, and real-world assets showing strong user engagement. He suggested that the integration of AI technology could lead to intelligent agents playing intermediary roles in trading, thereby increasing overall adoption. Users with low-risk preferences might become the primary source of growth in the future.

data.title: The BIO team transferred approximately $5.03 million worth of tokens to an exchange.
data.description: According to crypto analyst Ai (@ai_9684xtpa), BIO has surged 118% in the past week. About 11 hours ago, the project team transferred 120 million BIO tokens from a multi-signature address to three addresses, with approximately 80 million tokens going to OKX and Binance, worth about $5.031 million.

data.title: Dragonfly Partner: Retail investors have largely exited the market, and institutions are forming the bottom for Bitcoin prices.
data.description: Haseeb Qureshi, a partner at Dragonfly, stated that retail investors have largely exited the current crypto market, with institutions acting as the market bottom and supporting Bitcoin's price. He pointed out that Bitcoin is being viewed as a mature asset with sustained growth potential over the next 15 to 20 years, while altcoins need a clear narrative to regain retail attention. He also stated that the core of the crypto industry remains money and financial scenarios, with DeFi, stablecoins, exchanges, and RWA exhibiting strong user stickiness. With the integration of AI technology, intelligent agents may play an intermediary role in transactions, thereby increasing overall adoption, with low-risk-averse users potentially becoming the main source of incremental growth.

data.title: SEC Chair Paul Atkins Criticizes Outdated U.S. Crypto Legal Framework
data.description: SEC Chair Paul Atkins has expressed concerns about the current U.S. crypto legal framework, stating that it is outdated and does not align with the industry's development. According to NS3.AI, Atkins highlighted that U.S. elections over the past decade have significantly influenced the crypto regulatory landscape. He also mentioned that the SEC is collaborating with the CFTC to advance new legislation aimed at addressing these issues.

data.title: US senators reach compromise on stablecoin yield terms, potentially removing obstacles to the Clarity Act's progress.
data.description: US lawmakers have reached an agreement on stablecoin yield terms, a point of contention that had stalled the Clarity Act in the Senate for months. Senators Tom Tillis and Angela Alsobrooks have finalized the text of a compromise, which includes Section 404 prohibiting crypto companies from offering interest or yields economically or functionally equivalent to bank deposits, but allowing incentives related to use on legitimate platforms. Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated that the Senate Banking Committee should be urged to expedite the bill's review. This progress could pave the way for a previously stalled review process. (The Block)

data.title: B.AI Integrates GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.7 Models
data.description: B.AI has officially completed the integration and deployment of the next-generation models GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.7 as of May 1. According to ChainCatcher, developers and users can now directly access these advanced models via the platform's chat interface. GPT-5.5 offers significant improvements in reasoning, contextual understanding, and multimodal interaction, while Claude Opus 4.7 excels in code generation, logical alignment, and executing complex tasks. This upgrade aims to provide cutting-edge AI computational support to users within the ecosystem, reduce the barriers for developers to access top-tier models, and ensure higher response speed and stability.

data.title: BAIclaw Launches New AI Feature for Enhanced Web3 Trading
data.description: BAIclaw has officially launched the 'Sun Ge Brain' feature as of May 1, allowing users to access the 'Justin Sun Perspective' skill for an immersive experience in advanced language models and Web3 trading logic. According to ChainCatcher, this update signifies B.AI's continued efforts in integrating AI with on-chain ecosystems. The platform has also introduced Plan Pro and Plan Max subscription packages to support deeper on-chain interactions, offering enhanced execution bandwidth and a smoother trading experience.

data.title: Vietnam's April Exports Increase by 21% Year-on-Year
data.description: Vietnam's exports in April rose by 21% compared to the same period last year, surpassing the expected growth rate of 17.90%, according to Jin10. This marks an increase from the previous month's growth rate of 20.10%.
